pending.body: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:49-76` does not reject unknown or cross-variant fields during deserialization. Because `FindingClassification` is an internally tagged enum without any `deny_unknown_fields` guard, a payload like `{"classification":"fix-now",...,"mapped_to_bead_id":"bead-2"}` will deserialize as `FixNow` and silently discard the planned-elsewhere field. That violates the stated rule that each finding must have exactly one classification and lets malformed reviewer output take the wrong downstream path instead of being rejected.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:127-143` never validates `remediation_hint` when it is present on `FixNow`. `Some("")` and whitespace-only hints currently pass `validate_classification`, even though the bead explicitly calls for rejecting malformed classifications and empty-string edge cases. That means persisted/classified findings can carry a meaningless remediation hint as if it were valid data.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 5 -> Plan and Implement
